sanskrit: श्री भगवानुवाचसुदुर्दर्शमिदं रूपं दृष्टवानसि यन्मम । देवा अप्यस्य रूपस्य नित्यं दर्शनकाङ्क्षिणः ॥
hindiTranslation: श्रीभगवान् बोले  मेरा यह जो रूप तुमने देखा है, इसके दर्शन अत्यन्त ही दुर्लभ हैं ।  इस रूपको देखनेके लिये देवता भी नित्य लालायित रहते हैं ॥
englishTranslation: Krishna said: The form you have seen is very hard to behold. Even the radiant beings long forever to see it.
essence: The vision Arjuna saw is beyond ordinary access, even for radiant beings.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Krishna says Arjuna has seen a form that is extremely rare to behold. Even the radiant beings always long to see it.
simpleInsight: Some truths are so vast that even the highest beings long for them.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, after Arjuna has been overwhelmed by Krishna's cosmic form and then steadied by Krishna's gentler form, Krishna explains what Arjuna has just been allowed to see. The vision is not ordinary; it is rarer than anything even the radiant beings can behold.
modernRelevance: You catch a glimpse of something that changes you — a raw truth, a deep love, a moment of awe — and later realize most people never see it at all.
translationLiteral: श्रीभगवानुवाच (the Lord said) / सुदुर्दर्शम् (very hard to behold) / इदम् (this) / रूपम् (form) / दृष्टवान् (seen) / असि (you have) / यत् (which) / मम (of mine) / देवाः (radiant beings) / अपि (even) / अस्य (of this) / रूपस्य (of form) / नित्यम् (always) / दर्शनकाङ्क्षिणः (desiring to see)
insight: It shifts the mind from taking sacred experience for granted to recognising its rarity and value.
